title: viola canina subsp. contracta
intro: Viola canina subsp. contracta, also known as the dwarf or short-stemmed dog violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. contracta include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its ability to support wound healing and immune function. Viola canina subsp. contracta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
